actionUnits.condition: Auto-on propagation test. MonoBehaviour (script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) has fields: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, minNeighborDownDistance=3, downMultiplier=2. Ensure at least two neighboring Fire objects are within 4 units (one optionally >3 units lower in Y to test downMultiplier), and that FindNeighbors has been called so neighbors[] is populated. Expect shouldTurnOn=true then after timeToOn elapses, isOn=true, pSystem.Play(), neighbors notified via NotifyNeighborState(true), FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1).

Extinguish-by-water test. Requires external water system to call IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3). Repeated hits must occur with intervals < maxHitInterval (2s) until currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s). Example: call WaterHit every 1s for 6s. Expect state transition: isTurningOff=true while hitting; then isOn=false, shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0, pSystem.Stop(), neighbors notified via NotifyNeighborState(false), FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Hit-interval timeout test. With fire currently on, call WaterHit(Vector3) once, then do not hit again and wait > maxHitInterval (2s). Expect: isTurningOff becomes false after interval, currentHitTime reset to 0, fire remains on (no UpdateFireState(false) change).

Neighbor-threshold cancel test. Reduce onNeighbors below onNeighborsThreshold while this fire is off and shouldTurnOn==true (timer running). This requires a neighbor to notify NotifyNeighborState(false) or be removed from neighbors[] by external system. Expect: shouldTurnOn=false and timeToOnDelta reset to 0 (CheckNeighborState path for onNeighbors < threshold).
